text:
In the bustling town hall park, a colorful bird with keen eyes darted here and there. It was a cardinal, bright crimson feathers shimmering in the sunlight. Its curiosity stirred when a squirrel scurried down a tree trunk.

"Do-gooders," thought the cardinal. "Maybe it knows something I don't?"

It flew down to the ground and perched on a branch beside the squirrel.

The squirrel paused.

"Hey!" It chuckled. "What brings you down to this neck of the woods?"

"Information-seeking adventures," declared the cardinal grandly. "But first, tell me... do you?"

The squirrel nibbled on an acorn. "Well," it said thoughtfully, "I've learned that some berries are poisonous."

"Interesting," mused the cardinal. "How'd you learn that?"

"A friend taught me once. She had some funny aunts like that."

The cardinal tilted its head. "How interesting!"

Together they inspected several berry bushes. After some careful
